title: AQUA/MODIS Sea Surface Temperature (Nighttime, 4 micron) Daily L3m — Red Sea
abstract: Daily NASA AQUA/MODIS Level-3 mapped (L3m) nighttime sea surface temperature granules (SST4 product, 4-micron shortwave-infrared retrieval, unaffected by daytime sun-glint/reflection) covering the Red Sea, archived as a directory of daily NetCDF granules, a tar bundle of the same granules, and a single time-merged 'combined' NetCDF file.

spatial.resolution: 4 km (L3m daily, scene extent varies with swath/cloud cover)

resources.name: Daily SST4 (nighttime) granules (directory of NetCDF files)

variables.name: sst4
variables.label: Sea surface temperature (nighttime, 4um)
variables.description: Nighttime sea surface temperature retrieved from the 4-micron shortwave-infrared bands of AQUA/MODIS, standard L3m daily SST4 product (filename pattern AQUA_MODIS.YYYYMMDD.L3m.DAY.SST4.x_sst4.nc; recent files carry a .NRT near-real-time flag).

variables.name: sst4
variables.label: Sea surface temperature (nighttime, 4um)
variables.description: Same content as the extracted directory resource; tar-packaged copy of daily AQUA/MODIS L3m nighttime SST4 granules.

variables.name: sst4
variables.label: Sea surface temperature (nighttime, 4um)
variables.description: Apparent time-concatenated merge of the daily nighttime SST4 granules into one multi-time-step NetCDF file. Internal structure not verified - file was not opened/parsed.

steward.name: Unassigned - KAUST Red Sea Data Team

access.how_to_request: Contact KAUST Red Sea data team; underlying granules are also freely re-downloadable from NASA OceanColor Web (OB.DAAC) for the same date range and bounding box.

provenance.originator: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, Ocean Biology Processing Group (OB.DAAC) - AQUA/MODIS
provenance.citation: NASA Ocean Biology Processing Group. AQUA/MODIS Ocean Color Data, Sea Surface Temperature (4um nighttime, SST4), NASA OB.DAAC.

known_issues: n_rows for the combined NetCDF is inferred from the granule count of the daily-file resources, not verified by opening the file.
Files after ~2026-01 carry a .NRT (near-real-time) suffix and may later be replaced by reprocessed standard files.
